properties.remark: SEVERAL TREES UPROOTED, ONE OF WHICH FELL ON AND DAMAGED A HOME. SHED DESTROYED.
A FIFTY FOOT TREE FELL ONTO A TRAILER.
NINE POWER POLES KNOCKED DOWN AS WELL AS TWO CROSSARMS.
*** 1 INJ *** WINDS GUSTING OVER 60 MPH CREATED A DUST STORM. THERE WERE FIVE DISTINCT CRASHES INVOLVING FIVE CARS AND ONE SEMI-TRAILER ON A 1.5 MILE STRETCH OF SR-21
LARGE UTILITY POLES KNOCKED DOWN DUE TO STRONG WINDS ON RIVER ROAD. TRAFFIC DIVERTED AROUND THE AREA, MULTIPLE POWER OUTAGES.
WINDOWS BROKEN AND PAVILIONS DAMAGED BY STRONG THUNDERSTORM WINDS AT ANTELOPE ISLAND STATE PARK.
GREAT SALT LAKE MINERALS SENSOR, GSLM.
WINDS FROM A DRY MICROBURST KNOCKED OVER A SECTION OF FENCE AND CAUSED DAMAGE TO LARGE TREE LIMBS IN CLEARFIELD.
HILL AIR FORCE BASE SENSOR. WINDS CAUSED BY DRY MICROBURST MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.
CAUSEWAY SENSOR IN THE DUGWAY PROVING GROUND MESONET, DPG18.
APG SENSOR IN THE DUGWAY PROVING GROUND MESONET, DPG30.
US-189 AT DEER CREEK DAM SENSOR, UTDCD.
UDOT SENSOR AT KIMBALL JUNCTION I80, UT. KIJ
FLASH FLOOD REPORTED IN THE TOWN OF KANAB AND IN KANAB CREEK. OVER 2 INCHES OF PRECIPITATION WAS MEASURED.
FLASH FLOOD IMPACTING COHAB CANYON, SULPHUR CREEK, AND GRAND WASH.
US-50 CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
FLASH FLOODING OVER CLARION COUNTY ROAD SW OF CENTERFIELD. RADAR ESTIMATES OF 1.0-1.5 INCHES OF RAINFALL IN A ONE HOUR PERIOD.
MULTIPLE MUDSLIDES CLOSED NINE MILE CANYON ROAD. RADAR ESTIMATED 2.0-2.4 INCHES OF RAIN IN THIS AREA IN ABOUT 90 MINUTES.
FLASH FLOOD REPORTED ALONG THE PARIA RIVER.
MINOR FLASH FLOOD IN WAHWEAP CREEK.
ESCALANTE RIVER NEAR ESCALANTE ROSE FROM NEAR 1 CFS TO 1550 CFS.
4 TO 6 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER REPORTED IN CAPITOL GORGE, WHICH DAMAGED THE DIRT ROAD INTO THE AREA.
STATE ROUTES 143 AND 271 CLOSED DUE TO DEBRIS ON THE ROAD FROM FLASH FLOODING.
FLOODING REPORTED ACROSS THE LOWER HALF OF THE PARK FROM ROUGHLY CANYON JUNCTION SOUTHWARD INCLUDING PINE CREEK. FLOODING ALSO REPORTED IN THE TOWNS OF SPRINGDALE AND ROCKVILLE.
BOULDERES ACROSS THE ROAD ALONG STATE ROUTE 143 NEAR WHITE BRIDGE CAMPGROUND IN THE PANGUITCH CREEK DRAINAGE. A FRONT LOADER WAS NEEDED TO REMOVE THE BOULDERS.
FLASH FLOOD REPORTED ALONG COYOTE CREEK, WHICH IS A TRIBUTARY OF WAHWEAP CREEK.
PARIA RIVER ROSE FROM NEAR 7 CFS TO 1240 CFS, OR AN INCREASE OF NEARLY 3 FEET.
SR-143 CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING AND DEBRIS.
NB I-15 CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING BETWEEN MP 71 AND PAROWAN. MUCH OF OLD US HWY 91 WAS CLOSED AS WELL.
DEBRIS FLOW OFF OF THE BRIAN HEAD BURN SCAR BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 2 AND 14 CLOSED STATE ROUTE 143 IN BOTH DIRECTIONS.
RIGHT LANE OF NB I-15 CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING ON PAROWAN CREEK.
BROADCAST MEDIA TWEETED A PICTURE OF QUARTER SIZE HAIL IN HERRIMAN. LOCATION AND TIME ARE ESTIMATED.
*** 1 INJ *** MULTIPLE REPORTS OF HOUSEBOAT DAMAGE ON LAKE POWELL IN PADRE BAY AT GUNSIGHT VIEW. AT LEAST ONE ASSOCIATED INJURY REPORTED.
OBSERVATION FROM BULLFROG MARINA AUTOMATED WEATHER STATION.
MEASURED AT HENRY MOUNTAIN RAWS. OBSERVATION ARRIVED AT 357PM DUE TO HOURLY REPORTING, BUT RADAR INDICATES CONVECTIVE LINE PASSED AT 305PM.
LARGE FLASH FLOOD IN BUCKSKIN WASH AND SURROUNDING DRAINAGES.
FLASH FLOODING REPORTED IN MULTIPLE SLOT CANYONS IN ZION NATIONAL PARK.
FLASH FLOODING REPORTED ACROSS MANY TRIBUTARIES TO THE ESCALANTE RIVER AREA.
WIDESPREAD FLASH FLOODING ACROSS CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK.
FLASH FLOODING REPORTED IN BUCKSKIN WASH.
FLASH FLOODING REPORTED ON PLEASANT CREEK IN CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK.
